Keywords: three-tier, web, app, database, classic, enterprise
Description: Traditional web application with presentation app and data tiers
Components: Load Balancer, Web Servers, App Servers, Database, Cache
Use Cases: Enterprise applications, e-commerce, CMS, traditional web apps
Benefits: Clear separation of concerns, scalable, well-understood pattern
Considerations: May be overkill for simple apps, consider serverless for variable loads
Example Architecture: ALB -> EC2 Auto Scaling (Web) -> EC2 Auto Scaling (App) -> RDS Multi-AZ + ElastiCache

Keywords: microservices, kubernetes, k8s, eks, aks, gke, container
Description: Container-based microservices with service mesh
Components: Kubernetes Cluster, Ingress Controller, Service Mesh, Container Registry, Secrets Management
Use Cases: Microservices, cloud-native apps, high scalability requirements
Benefits: Independent scaling, technology flexibility, resilience
Considerations: Operational complexity, requires K8s expertise, distributed tracing needed
Example Architecture: EKS Cluster -> ALB Ingress -> Services (Istio mesh) -> Pods -> RDS/DynamoDB

Keywords: serverless, lambda, api-gateway, functions, event-driven
Description: Event-driven serverless architecture with managed services
Components: API Gateway, Lambda Functions, DynamoDB/Cosmos, S3/Blob, SQS/Service Bus
Use Cases: APIs, event processing, variable workloads, cost-sensitive projects
Benefits: Pay-per-use, auto-scaling, no server management, fast development
Considerations: Cold starts, execution limits, vendor lock-in, debugging complexity
Example Architecture: API Gateway -> Lambda -> DynamoDB + S3 + SQS

Keywords: data-lake, analytics, big-data, s3, gcs, etl, warehouse
Description: Centralized data repository for analytics and ML
Components: Object Storage, ETL/Glue, Data Catalog, Query Engine, BI Tools
Use Cases: Big data analytics, ML/AI pipelines, business intelligence
Benefits: Schema-on-read flexibility, cost-effective storage, multiple query engines
Considerations: Data governance complexity, requires data engineering expertise
Example Architecture: S3 (Raw/Processed/Curated) -> Glue ETL -> Glue Catalog -> Athena/Redshift

Keywords: multi-region, active-active, global, disaster-recovery, ha
Description: Globally distributed application with active instances in multiple regions
Components: Global Load Balancer, Regional Clusters, Database Replication, CDN
Use Cases: Global applications, zero-downtime requirements, data sovereignty
Benefits: Highest availability, low latency globally, regional compliance
Considerations: Complexity, cost, data consistency challenges, conflict resolution
Example Architecture: Route53 (Latency) -> CloudFront -> Regional ALB -> Regional EKS -> Aurora Global

Keywords: multi-region, active-passive, dr, failover, backup
Description: Primary region with standby region for disaster recovery
Components: Primary Infrastructure, Standby Infrastructure, Replication, DNS Failover
Use Cases: Business continuity, compliance requirements, cost-effective DR
Benefits: Lower cost than active-active, clear failover process
Considerations: RTO/RPO tradeoffs, failover testing required, potential data loss
Example Architecture: Primary: Full Stack -> Secondary: Warm Standby -> Route53 Health Check Failover

Keywords: hub-spoke, transit, networking, vpn, peering, firewall
Description: Centralized network topology with shared services hub
Components: Transit Gateway/Hub VNet, Spoke VPCs/VNets, Firewall, VPN Gateway
Use Cases: Multi-account/subscription environments, centralized security, shared services
Benefits: Centralized control, simplified connectivity, security inspection
Considerations: Single point of failure, traffic costs, complexity scaling
Example Architecture: Transit Gateway Hub -> Spoke VPCs (Dev/Staging/Prod) -> Shared Services VPC

Keywords: landing-zone, foundation, account, organization, governance
Description: Enterprise-ready cloud foundation with governance
Components: Organization, Accounts/Subscriptions, IAM, Networking, Logging, Security
Use Cases: Enterprise cloud adoption, multi-team environments, compliance
Benefits: Governance from day one, security baseline, scalable structure
Considerations: Initial investment, ongoing maintenance, requires cloud expertise
Example Architecture: Organization -> Core OUs -> Accounts (Security/Log/Shared) -> Spoke Accounts

Keywords: gitops, argocd, flux, ci-cd, automation, pipeline
Description: Infrastructure managed through Git with automated deployment
Components: Git Repository, CI/CD Pipeline, Terraform Cloud/Atlantis, State Backend
Use Cases: Mature teams, audit requirements, collaborative infrastructure
Benefits: Version control, audit trail, review process, rollback capability
Considerations: Learning curve, pipeline maintenance, secret management
Example Architecture: GitHub -> Atlantis/TF Cloud -> Plan/Apply -> AWS/Azure/GCP

Keywords: event-driven, messaging, sns, sqs, eventbridge, pub-sub
Description: Loosely coupled services communicating through events
Components: Event Bus, Message Queues, Lambda/Functions, Event Store
Use Cases: Real-time processing, decoupled services, async workflows
Benefits: Loose coupling, scalability, resilience, flexibility
Considerations: Eventual consistency, debugging complexity, event schema management
Example Architecture: EventBridge -> Lambda (Processor) -> SQS -> Lambda (Worker) -> DynamoDB

Keywords: static, cdn, s3, cloudfront, jamstack, spa
Description: Static content served globally through CDN
Components: Object Storage, CDN, DNS, SSL Certificate, WAF (optional)
Use Cases: Static websites, SPAs, marketing sites, documentation
Benefits: Low cost, high performance, global reach, no server management
Considerations: Limited dynamic functionality, build process required
Example Architecture: S3 (Static) -> CloudFront (CDN) -> Route53 -> ACM (SSL)

Keywords: container, registry, ecr, acr, gcr, pipeline, docker
Description: Container image build and storage pipeline
Components: Source Control, Build Pipeline, Container Registry, Vulnerability Scanning
Use Cases: Container deployments, microservices, CI/CD pipelines
Benefits: Automated builds, vulnerability scanning, versioned images
Considerations: Registry costs, image pruning, security scanning configuration
Example Architecture: GitHub -> CodeBuild/Cloud Build -> ECR/GCR (Scan) -> EKS/GKE

Keywords: zero-trust, security, identity, access, network, bastion
Description: Identity-based access with no implicit trust
Components: Identity Provider, Access Proxy, Private Endpoints, Logging
Use Cases: High-security environments, remote workforce, compliance
Benefits: Strong security posture, reduced attack surface, auditability
Considerations: User experience friction, implementation complexity
Example Architecture: IAM Identity Center -> Private Access -> VPC Endpoints -> Private Resources

Keywords: immutable, ami, image, blue-green, rolling, deployment
Description: Infrastructure replaced rather than modified in place
Components: Image Builder, Auto Scaling, Load Balancer, Blue-Green Setup
Use Cases: Consistent deployments, easy rollbacks, reduced drift
Benefits: Predictable deployments, easy rollbacks, no configuration drift
Considerations: Image build time, storage costs, requires automation
Example Architecture: Packer -> AMI -> Launch Template -> ASG (Blue/Green) -> ALB

Keywords: hybrid, vpn, direct-connect, expressroute, on-premises
Description: Connection between cloud and on-premises infrastructure
Components: VPN Gateway, Direct Connect/ExpressRoute, DNS Resolution, Firewall
Use Cases: Enterprise with on-prem, gradual migration, data residency
Benefits: Leverage existing investments, gradual migration, data locality
Considerations: Complexity, latency, bandwidth costs, security boundaries
Example Architecture: On-Prem DC <-> Direct Connect/VPN <-> Transit Gateway <-> Cloud VPCs

Keywords: monitoring, logging, tracing, metrics, observability, datadog
Description: Complete observability with metrics logs and traces
Components: Metrics (Prometheus/CloudWatch), Logs (ELK/CloudWatch), Traces (X-Ray/Jaeger), Dashboards
Use Cases: Production systems, microservices, troubleshooting
Benefits: Full visibility, faster debugging, performance insights
Considerations: Data volume costs, alert fatigue, correlation complexity
Example Architecture: CloudWatch/Prometheus -> Grafana + ELK/CloudWatch Logs + X-Ray/Jaeger

Keywords: cost, spot, reserved, savings, optimization, budget
Description: Infrastructure optimized for cost efficiency
Components: Spot/Preemptible Instances, Reserved Capacity, Auto Scaling, Lifecycle Policies
Use Cases: Development environments, batch processing, cost-sensitive workloads
Benefits: Significant cost savings, automated optimization
Considerations: Instance interruptions, capacity availability, complexity
Example Architecture: Spot Fleet (80%) + On-Demand (20%) + Auto Scaling + S3 Lifecycle + Budget Alerts

Keywords: compliance, hipaa, soc2, pci, gdpr, audit
Description: Infrastructure meeting regulatory compliance requirements
Components: Encryption, Logging, Access Controls, Audit Trail, Backup/Recovery
Use Cases: Healthcare, financial services, government, regulated industries
Benefits: Compliance out of the box, audit-ready, reduced risk
Considerations: Complexity, cost, ongoing maintenance, expertise required
Example Architecture: KMS Encryption + CloudTrail + Config Rules + GuardDuty + Backup Plans

Keywords: api, gateway, management, rate-limiting, authentication
Description: Managed API platform with security and monitoring
Components: API Gateway, Authentication, Rate Limiting, Monitoring, Documentation
Use Cases: API products, partner integrations, public APIs
Benefits: Centralized management, security, analytics, developer experience
Considerations: Vendor lock-in, latency overhead, cost at scale
Example Architecture: API Gateway -> Lambda Authorizer -> Backend Services -> CloudWatch

Keywords: ml, machine-learning, sagemaker, vertexai, mlops
Description: End-to-end ML platform for training and inference
Components: Notebook Environment, Training Infrastructure, Model Registry, Inference Endpoints
Use Cases: ML/AI projects, data science teams, model deployment
Benefits: Managed infrastructure, scalable training, easy deployment
Considerations: Cost management, data governance, model versioning
Example Architecture: SageMaker Studio -> Training Jobs -> Model Registry -> Endpoints
